title: agent-ssh transport-provider contract
description: The contract every transport module registers against. A transport is its OWN plugin (in its audience's marketplace) that ships a module.yaml conforming to this schema. agent-ssh's generic core consumes it to emit name-keyed SSH profiles. PUBLIC contract; transport instances (Cloudflare, dtssh) live in their own repos.

module.description: Transport name. Namespaces the drop-in fragment (~/.ssh/config.d/50-agent-ssh-<module>.conf) and the registry `transport:` dispatch key.

proxy_command.description: OPTIONAL recipe: the ProxyCommand template used to dial a host. Placeholders: {hostname} {name} {user} {port} {proxy_binary}. Omit for a plain-SSH transport (e.g. `direct`). This is the ONLY transport-specific rendering the core needs.

proxy_binary_default.description: Default binary substituted into {proxy_binary} when the registry does not override `proxy_command_binary` (e.g. 'cloudflared'). Transport-owned; never hardcoded in the core.

inbound_ports.description: Firewall posture. 'none' => zero-inbound tunnel (preferred, tunnel-first default). 'some' => a deliberately-chosen direct/listening transport.

capabilities.description: Which lifecycle verbs the transport implements. emit-profile + verify are satisfied by the core; install-client and provision-server are transport-owned.

install-client.description: Transport installs a client binary. On Windows the install-client entrypoint MUST place a real standalone exe on PATH ahead of WinGet\Links (NOT a WinGet App Execution Alias shim, which fails over non-interactive SSH). See docs/transport-provider-contract.md.

config_schema.description: Path (within the transport plugin) to the transport's own config schema, extending contract/registry-record.schema.json.

entrypoints.description: Transport-owned scripts the core shells to for verbs the core does not itself implement (install-client, provision-server). Dual posix/windows per cross-platform-parity.
